title: oxalis oregana
intro: Oxalis oregana, also known as the Oregon oxalis or mountain woods sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family and is native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and antiseptic qualities, which can help to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Oxalis oregana include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Oxalis oregana can be prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
